name: Cinematic 4-Screen Pattern (Adam Lyttle)

fields.definition_origin: Codified by Adam Lyttle, indie iOS developer (multiple top-grossing utility apps including Widgetable, Locket alternatives, custom keyboards) in his 2023-2024 YouTube essays and Twitter/X teardowns ('The App Onboarding Secrets That Convert' video, summarized at infocaptor.com). Lyttle articulated the 4-screen format specifically for utility/single-feature apps where personalization adds nothing — Welcome / Social Proof / Core Demo / Paywall, each fullscreen and animated. The pattern's predecessors include 1990s-era infomercial structure (problem → testimonial → demo → call-to-action) and the AIDA copywriting framework (Awareness / Interest / Desire / Action — E. St. Elmo Lewis 1898). What's distinctive about Lyttle's framework is the EXPLICIT pairing with hard paywall at screen 4 (no skip), full-bleed animated photography/video on screens 1-3, and the constraint that the entire flow targets <60 seconds before paywall ask. Strongly resonant with Mobbin teardowns of utility category (Widgetable, AI Photo, lock-screen widget apps) and the indie-app community on X.
fields.core_mechanism: Operates on the inverse-personalization principle: when category value is universally understood, every personalization screen is (a) leakage from the funnel, and (b) wasted opportunity to deliver value-promise → social proof → demo → ask. Mechanism stack: (1) AIDA arc — Welcome (Awareness/Attention), Social Proof (Interest), Core Demo (Desire), Paywall (Action). (2) Cinematic full-bleed visuals trigger affective evaluation (Damasio somatic markers) faster than text-led screens. (3) Single CTA per screen reduces decision load (Hick's Law, item 8). (4) Hard paywall at screen 4 leverages momentum + low cognitive investment to convert before the user has chance to disengage. (5) Speed: <60s end-to-end means the user purchases on impulse rather than considered evaluation — the value prop must be self-evident to the user before they install (UGC creative ↔ onboarding alignment, item 42). (6) Specifically does NOT use sunk-cost (item 17) or identity arc (item 18) — both require time investment the cinematic format refuses to take. (7) Trade-off: works only when value prop is universal AND App Store install intent is strong (the user already knows why they're here). For novel categories or low-intent installs, this pattern fails — long onboarding wins.
fields.neuroscience_correlate: Cinematic visuals engage ventral visual stream + amygdala for emotional response (LeDoux 1996). Full-bleed photography activates the IT cortex / fusiform face area more strongly than UI illustrations. Social-proof screen engages mirror neuron + medial prefrontal social-cognition systems. [uncertain on direct mobile-onboarding fMRI]
fields.empirical_evidence: (1) Adam Lyttle's apps (multiple top-grossing utility/widget apps) consistently use 4-screen cinematic — his YouTube and X content cite revenue scaling without long onboarding. Specific revenue figures self-reported on YouTube. (2) Widgetable, AI Photo, lock-screen widget apps in 2023-2024 utility cohort cluster around 4-screen onboarding (Mobbin / SensorTower observation). (3) Adam Lyttle's infocaptor summary explicitly catalogs the format with examples. (4) RevenueCat 2025 State of Subscription Apps: utility apps cluster at high direct-purchase rate (low trial conversion, but high day-0 buy) — supports 4-screen-to-paywall hypothesis. (5) Adapty 2026 paywall report: short-onboarding apps still hit the 1.35% onboarding-paywall conversion benchmark when value prop is strong + paywall is hard. (6) Mau Baron's framework explicitly EXCLUDES utilities from his story-onboarding doctrine — affirms category boundary that 4-screen owns. (7) Counter-evidence: AI-wrapper apps with weak universal value (no clear pre-install schema) underperform with 4-screen vs long onboarding — RevenueCat 2025 5.31% AI-wrapper trial conversion vs 10.92% average suggests personalization helps in AI categories. (8) Foundational AIDA framework (Lewis 1898; Strong 1925) replicated continuously in marketing research.
fields.measurable_outcome_metric: Time-to-paywall (target <60s), screen-by-screen completion rate (target ≥85% per screen, ≥60% reach paywall), Day-0 trial-start or direct-purchase rate (THE headline metric — utility apps lean direct purchase), refund rate (high refund signals over-promised cinematic), App Store conversion (cinematic onboarding implies strong creative match — measure CPI vs conversion).
fields.evidence_strength: case_study + practitioner_opinion (Lyttle's specific framework is single-practitioner-articulated; underlying AIDA is foundational marketing)
fields.conversion_lift_benchmark: unverified at peer-reviewed RCT level. Adam Lyttle self-reported (YouTube, X 2023-2024): 4-screen format outperforms long onboarding for his utility category — specific lift figures vary by app. Adapty 2026 indirectly: short-onboarding utility apps achieve ~1.35% onboarding-paywall conversion when value prop strong; comparable to long-onboarding apps for the same conversion event but at much lower onboarding length / cost.
fields.engineering_tactics: (1) FSM with exactly 4 states: WELCOME → SOCIAL_PROOF → DEMO → PAYWALL. No skip until screen 4. (2) Full-bleed visual treatment per screen — fullscreen photo/video at 100% opacity, copy overlaid in lower 1/3 (text legibility scrim if needed). Lottie / Rive / native video for animation. (3) Cabinet Grotesk Bold 32-40px headline per screen, 1-line ideally; supporting copy 17px Söhne Regular max 2 lines. (4) Single primary CTA per screen, full-width 56px tall, bottom inset 24px (thumb zone). (5) NO progress indicator (4 screens too short — implies length). (6) Page transition: cubic-bezier(0.32, 0.72, 0, 1) at 320ms slide-from-right. (7) Screen 1 (Welcome): brand mark + outcome headline (5-9 words), animated background loop. (8) Screen 2 (Social Proof): testimonial card, star rating, '5M+ users' or App Store review quote — must be SUBSTANTIATED (FTC § 5 risk if fabricated). (9) Screen 3 (Core Demo): video or interactive preview of THE feature — 3-5 second loop showing the magic. (10) Screen 4 (Paywall): hard paywall, side-by-side plan selection (post-toggle-ban Apple Guideline 3.1.2 effective 2026-01) — ${weekly with 3-day trial vs annual} typically. (11) Telemetry: emit `cinematic_screen_viewed { screen_id }`, `cinematic_screen_completed { screen_id, time_on_screen_ms }`, `cinematic_paywall_shown`, `cinematic_paywall_purchase | dismiss`. (12) Localization: full-bleed visuals must be localized (testimonials especially); same-asset across locales reads inauthentic. (13) Dark-mode: cinematic visuals must work in both modes — test cinematic photography against dark backdrops. (14) Performance: video files keep under 2MB each; pre-load on app launch; provide low-data-mode fallback to image. (15) UGC creative alignment (item 42) — first screen must mirror the ad the user clicked.
fields.real_world_examples: (1) Widgetable (utility, lock-screen widgets) — 4-screen cinematic with full-bleed widget previews, paywall at screen 4 (Mobbin teardown). (2) AI Photo (utility, AI-generated portraits) — Welcome (cinematic before/after) → Social Proof (5M users) → Demo (animated transformation) → Paywall. (3) Locket alternatives — short cinematic onboarding, paywall after 3-4 screens. (4) AllTrails older onboarding — 4 cinematic screens of trail visuals before paywall. (5) Photoroom early onboarding — Welcome → Social Proof → Demo → Paywall in cinematic format. (6) Many TikTok-driven indie utilities (custom keyboard apps, ringtone apps, weather apps) — Adam Lyttle's blueprint visible in dozens of Mobbin captures 2023-2024. (7) Ringy / NBA stat utilities — 4-screen cinematic + paywall. (8) Adam Lyttle's own apps (multiple top-grossing utility apps cited in his YouTube essays) — direct evidence. (9) Counter-example for contrast: Cal AI / Flo / Headspace explicitly DON'T use this — long onboarding wins for novel categories.
fields.mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile-primary — cinematic full-bleed exploits mobile's full-screen attention budget. Web equivalent: marketing landing page with hero video → social proof section → product demo → pricing CTA — same AIDA arc but rarely a literal '4 screens' (web is single-page-scroll). Mobile must use platform-native page transition (UIPageViewController, ViewPager2) for snappy navigation. Mobile cinematic video assets need compression carefully (low-data-mode fallback).
fields.platform_design_language_alignment: Liquid Glass (iOS 26+): bolder, left-aligned alert/onboarding typography is purpose-built for the 1-line cinematic headline. Translucent material lets the cinematic visual shine through scrim layers. M3 Expressive (Android 16+): expressive motion + shape supports the cinematic transitions; M3 explicitly endorses video-led onboarding for media-heavy categories. Both DLs reward CONFIDENT large-typography, full-bleed imagery — perfect alignment.
fields.category_fit: STRONGEST fit: utilities (widgets, weather, ringtones, custom keyboards, calculators), single-feature creative tools (Photoroom-like image edit, AI-photo apps), entertainment (one-feature games, novelty apps), media-consumption with universal value (Netflix-like). Acceptable fit: e-commerce (Wish-style impulse-buy onboarding), simple gaming. WEAK fit: wellness/habit (long onboarding wins — items 17/18), AI-wrapper consumer (personalization is the differentiator — long wins), B2B/productivity (no impulse purchase), fintech (KYC required), social (network effects need seeding). EXCLUSION: 'Do not use 4-screen cinematic for novel/AI-wrapper categories where the user doesn't pre-install with a clear schema of value — short onboarding fails.'
fields.solo_dev_feasibility: 1_week. Authoring 4 cinematic screens with full-bleed video/animation is the bulk — hire a videographer or use stock + custom animation. Engineering is trivial (4-state FSM, native page view). Adapty / RevenueCat / Superwall paywall integration is hours. Solo devs in indie utility space ship this pattern in days; quality of cinematic visuals is the bottleneck.
fields.tooling_options: RevenueCat / Adapty Pro / Superwall (paywall + screen 4 management with A/B testing), Lottie / Rive (animation), AVFoundation iOS / ExoPlayer Android (video playback), Unsplash / Envato (stock cinematic imagery), Plotline / Adapty Onboarding Builder (managed 4-screen + paywall A/B), custom React Native / SwiftUI / Compose for full control. App Store optimization (ASO) tools: AppFollow / Appfigures for screenshot alignment with onboarding cinematic.
fields.ai_dependency: none for the pattern itself; OPTIONAL for cinematic asset generation (Sora-class video models, Runway, Pika for cinematic video; Midjourney for stills) — be cautious of AI-slop tells (item .agent/research/emotional-design/results/AI_Slop_Tells.json). Always human-direct for brand consistency. State: none; optional generative AI for asset production with strong human curation.
fields.claude_design_application: Instruct Claude Design to build the 4-screen cinematic as an FSM with full-bleed visual primary screens. Prompt fragment: 'Build a CinematicOnboarding FSM in XState v5 with exactly 4 states: welcome → social_proof → demo → paywall. NO skip affordance until screen 4. Each screen is fullscreen (390x844) with: full-bleed photography or video (Lottie/Rive/AVFoundation), copy overlay in lower 1/3 with text-readability scrim if needed. Headline: Cabinet Grotesk Bold 32-40px, 1 line ideally, 5-9 words, outcome-led (NOT feature-led). Single primary CTA full-width 56px bottom inset 24px. Page transition: cubic-bezier(0.32, 0.72, 0, 1) at 320ms slide-from-right. Time-to-paywall target: <60s. Screen 4 (paywall): hard paywall side-by-side plans (post-toggle-ban Apple Guideline 3.1.2 effective 2026-01). UGC alignment: screen 1 must accept a `ad_variant` prop and route to ad-specific visual variant. Forbid: progress indicator, skip-to-end, multiple CTAs per screen, feature-list copy, Lucide icons, indigo gradient.'
fields.stitch_application: Prompt fragment: 'Mockup a 4-screen cinematic onboarding for a [widget/utility] app at 390x844 each. SCREEN 1 (Welcome): full-bleed photograph of [the core feature shown in context] (e.g., iPhone home screen with the widget visible), 32px Cabinet Grotesk Bold headline overlay lower-third "Make your home screen yours." with subtle dark scrim for legibility, brand mark top-left small, full-width primary CTA "Get Started" bottom inset 24px. SCREEN 2 (Social Proof): full-bleed photograph of happy user, large 5-star rating overlay (32px gold stars), pull-quote testimonial 24px Cabinet Grotesk Italic "This changed my home screen game" — Sarah K., 32px attribution, full-width CTA "Continue" bottom. SCREEN 3 (Demo): full-bleed video loop of the feature in action (3-5s loop), simple caption "Drag and drop. That\'s it." 28px headline, full-width CTA "Try it" bottom. SCREEN 4 (Paywall): side-by-side plan tiles (Weekly $4.99 with 3-day trial vs Annual $39.99 "Save 67%"), no toggle (banned), single "Continue" CTA, terms link below. Forbid: feature-tour cards, Lucide icon trinity, indigo gradient, generic stock photography of laughing-at-laptop.'
fields.ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI codegen typically generates this pattern WORST in two ways: (1) reverts to 7-10 screen feature-tour cards instead of 4-screen cinematic — over-builds, (2) uses generic stock photography (laughing-at-laptop, hands-on-keyboard) and Lucide icons instead of cinematic full-bleed photography/video. Specific failures: (3) feature-list copy on each screen instead of outcome headline, (4) progress dots indicating 4-screen length (defeats cinematic feel), (5) light/airy gradient backgrounds instead of cinematic full-bleed imagery, (6) toggle-paywall on screen 4 (banned post 2026-01), (7) skip affordance at every screen (defeats funnel), (8) indigo-purple-gradient default. Counter-prompt: 'CONSTRAINT: 4 screens exactly, no more no less. Each screen is FULLSCREEN with full-bleed photography or video — NOT illustrations, NOT Lucide icons, NOT gradients. Headline: outcome-led 5-9 words on each screen. Single primary CTA per screen. NO progress dots. NO skip until screen 4. Screen 4 paywall must use SIDE-BY-SIDE plan tiles (toggle banned post 2026-01). Forbid: feature-tour carousel format, generic stock photography (laughing-at-laptop), Lucide icons, indigo-purple-gradient. UGC alignment: screen 1 visual must mirror the ad creative.'
fields.anti_patterns: (1) Over-extending to 6-8 screens (becomes feature-tour, loses cinematic discipline). (2) Generic stock photography instead of cinematic on-brand imagery. (3) Feature-list copy ('AI-powered widgets!') instead of outcome ('Make your home screen yours'). (4) Skip-to-end affordance defeating funnel pressure. (5) Toggle paywall on screen 4 (banned 2026-01 — Apple Guideline 3.1.2). (6) Mismatch with ad creative (UGC alignment, item 42 — first screen must mirror ad). (7) Cinematic visuals that don't show the actual product (false advertising vibe). (8) Audio that auto-plays loud — accessibility + noise-shock. (9) 4-screen pattern applied to wrong category (wellness, AI-wrapper, B2B) where long onboarding wins. (10) Fabricated social proof on screen 2 (FTC § 5 deceptive practice).
fields.regulatory_risk: Medium. Hard paywall is allowed but Apple Guideline 3.1.2 (toggle-paywall ban effective 2026-01) requires side-by-side plans, not toggle. FTC § 5: social-proof screen 2 testimonials must be substantiated. FTC Click-to-Cancel (effective 2025-07-14): cancellation must be as easy as 4-screen sign-up — keep cancellation UX equally short. EU Digital Fairness Act (Q4 2026 draft) may flag short funnels with hard paywall as 'pressure tactics' — pair with clear pricing transparency on screen 4. ASA / ACCC / UK CMA scrutiny on outcome claims in cinematic visuals (e.g., 'lose 30 lbs' style headline subject to substantiation).
fields.platform_constraints_2026: Apple Guideline 3.1.2 (toggle-paywall ban effective 2026-01) — screen 4 paywall MUST use side-by-side plan selection or timeline transparency, NOT toggle UI. Apple Guideline 3.1.1 (IAP subscription disclosure) — pricing must be visible up-front. Apple Guideline 5.1.1(v) data minimization — 4-screen pattern naturally complies (no questionnaire). Android target API 35 + Play Subscription requirements. EU Digital Markets Act + Digital Services Act apply.
fields.accessibility_implications: WCAG 2.2: cinematic videos must have captions (1.2.2 Captions Prerecorded), audio descriptions if video conveys info via audio (1.2.5), prefers-reduced-motion replaces auto-play video with static (2.3.3 Animation from Interactions). Color contrast on copy-over-visual must meet 4.5:1 — use scrim or shadow if needed. Dynamic Type 200%: lower-1/3 copy must reflow without truncation (avoid fixed-pixel constraints). VoiceOver: full-bleed images need accessible labels (NOT 'image of...') describing the demonstrated outcome. Auto-play video can violate 1.4.2 Audio Control if sound is loud — mute by default, user-initiated unmute.
fields.pitfalls_caveats: (1) Cinematic visual cost — 4 screens of full-bleed photography/video is ~$2-10K for original work; using AI-generated visuals risks AI-slop tells. (2) Short funnel = high pressure on every screen; weak copy or visual on any screen tanks the entire flow. (3) Wrong category fit is the most common failure — utilities/single-feature work; wellness/AI-wrapper/B2B does not. (4) UGC alignment (item 42) is critical — if ad creative promises X and screen 1 shows Y, conversion crashes. (5) Localization of cinematic assets is expensive — original photography per locale ideal but rarely budgeted. (6) Apple App Store screenshot must match cinematic — alignment chain App Store → screen 1 of onboarding is non-negotiable. (7) Hard paywall on screen 4 demands strong substantiation if user later seeks refund — over-promised cinematic = high refund rate. (8) No personalization means no Day-0 LLM tokens — costs lower than long onboarding (zero AI dependency).
fields.cross_cultural_validity: Strong fit globally with localization. CN super-app context: 4-screen format works but social-proof screen should use Chinese testimonials, App Store ratings adapted. JP/KR: cinematic photography preferred over illustration; testimonials less aggressive. LATAM: warmer cinematic palette + community framing on social-proof screen. MENA: cinematic must be RTL-mirrored, modesty-aware imagery. India: pricing transparency on screen 4 critical (regional pricing expected). All locales: Apple StoreKit auto-conversion of price ≠ optimal — manually set local-currency price points. Adapty 2026 62.3% LTV win rate on localization tests applies — localize cinematic visuals not just translate.
fields.prescribed_action: For utility / single-feature / universal-value categories, use exactly 4 fullscreen cinematic screens — Welcome / Social Proof / Demo / Paywall — with time-to-paywall <60s. Each screen: full-bleed photography or video, outcome-led 5-9 word headline, single primary CTA. Screen 4 paywall must use side-by-side plan tiles (toggle banned 2026-01). UGC alignment: screen 1 mirrors the ad creative. DO NOT use this pattern for wellness, AI-wrapper, B2B, fintech, or social — long onboarding wins those categories.
fields.questionnaire_relevance: N/A — cinematic 4-screen explicitly avoids questionnaires. Personalization is sacrificed for speed-to-paywall. If category needs personalization (wellness, AI-wrapper), switch to long-onboarding (items 17/18).
fields.aha_relevance: Aha is engineered ON SCREEN 3 (Demo) — the 3-5 second video/animation IS the value demonstration. Time-to-Aha <30s. No AI Plan Reveal (item 12) needed because category value is universal — demo IS the reveal.
fields.measurement_horizon: Day_0 (paywall conversion is the headline) + Day_30 (refund rate — over-promised cinematic produces high refunds)
fields.emotional_archetype_alignment: Hero (cinematic action, 'do this'), Ruler (efficient, you know what you came for), Magician (transformation visualized in demo), Innocent (simple, clear, trustworthy). Mismatches: Caregiver (no warm extended onboarding to nurture), Sage (no time for explanation), Lover (no relationship-building). Cinematic aligns with high-energy, action-oriented archetypes.
fields.anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against Wall-of-Text + Cognitive Overload (Six-Antipattern #1) — explicit antipattern. Pairs against Toggle Paywall (banned 2026-01) when implemented correctly. Anti-pairs with Long Questionnaire (item 17) and Story Onboarding (item 18) — explicit category alternatives.
sources: [Adam Lyttle — The App Onboarding Secrets That Convert (Infocaptor summary)](https://my.infocaptor.com/hub/summaries/adam-lyttle/the-app-onboarding-secrets-that-convert-proven-strategies-QMo2T5apdYw)
[Apple App Store Review Guideline 3.1.2 — Subscription Disclosure (toggle-paywall ban effective 2026-01)](https://developer.apple.com/app-store/review/guidelines/#3.1.2)

uncertain: specific RCT-grade conversion-lift figures comparing 4-screen vs longer onboarding (case-study level only; Lyttle's data is self-reported)
